step_by_step_thinking:

Then write up your final agreement/disagreement in exactly two sentences as instructed:
Finally, give your clear answer choice in boxed format.
After reviewing the retrieved evidence, I confirm that colistin is explicitly described as belonging to the polymyxin class of antibiotics. Multiple sources identify colistin as a "member" or "derivative" of the polymyxin family and emphasize its role in treating severe infections caused by multidrug-resistant Gram-negative bacteria — consistent with established definitions of antibiotics. Despite concerns around nephrotoxicity and rising resistance, the evidence consistently categorizes colistin as an antibiotic within clinical guidelines and pharmacopeial classifications. There is no indication in any source that colistin fails to qualify as an antibiotic despite its side-effect profile or restricted use.
